text: :rotating_light: FIRING: High error rate on report-generation-service (>5% 5xx for 5m). Severity: P2. Runbook: https://wiki.internal/runbooks/report-gen
Grafana shows postgres-primary connection pool at 200/200. Something is holding all connections.
Found it — report job R-8821 started at 14:21 and is running a full table scan on the events table. 200M rows. This is what's eating the pool.
Confirmed. The events table is missing an index on tenant_id. The query planner is doing a seq scan. Probably worked fine when the table was smaller.
R-8821 killed. Connection pool draining — down to 45/200. Error rates dropping.
:white_check_mark: RESOLVED: High error rate on report-generation-service. Duration: 8 minutes.

I'll file a ticket for the index. Should be a composite on (tenant_id, created_at). Also — should we move scheduled jobs to a read replica? This would have been a non-event if the report job wasn't on primary.
Good call. Also wondering why the slow query alert didn't fire. The threshold might need a review. Adding both to action items.

Impact: roughly 3,200 users got 503s on /api/v1/reports during the 8 min window. No revenue impact confirmed yet — checking with product. Marking this as resolved. Post-mortem doc needed by EOD Friday.
